libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: For tests that call com.mchange.v2.naming.ReferenceableUtils.referenceToObject(...) without the allowedFactoryClassNames overload, set the system property com.mchange.v2.naming.objectFactoryWhitelist to the exact test ObjectFactory class name, for example com_mchange.mchange_commons_java.ReferenceableUtilsTest$CapturingObjectFactory, and clear/restore it after the test. Alternatively, pass an explicit allowed factory Set to the overload that accepts allowedFactoryClassNames. No Docker service is indicated. Optional POM dependencies such as com.typesafe:config, log4j, log4j2, and slf4j are only needed if tests deliberately cover those optional integrations, not for core utility coverage.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #2097 [Automation] java run fails for com.mchange:mchange-commons-java:0.4.0; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Using ReferenceableUtils.ALL_FACTORY_CLASS_NAMES or a broad whitelist would bypass the security behavior and weaken the value of the test.
If generated tests choose to exercise optional HOCON or logging bridge APIs, additional test dependencies may then be needed, but they are not required to fix the observed java-run failure.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: mchange-commons-java 0.4.0 adds a mandatory ObjectFactory whitelist for ReferenceableUtils overloads that do not receive an explicit allowed-factory set; tests exercising that normal JNDI helper path must configure it.
